step_by_step_thinking:
HBsAg is the earliest and direct indicator of an ongoing Hepatitis B infection because it represents the presence of the virus itself on its surface. While other markers like Anti-HBc IgM/IgG might suggest prior exposure or recent infection, HBsAg specifically points towards an active infection due to its association with the virion. Since we're dealing with a scenario involving a patient already confirmed to have Hepatitis B, focusing on what directly confirms the active infection makes sense here.
\boxed{A} Based on the information provided and considering that HBsAg is directly indicative of the presence of the Hepatitis B virus, making it the earliest signifier of an active infection, I conclude:
